---
pagetitle: "Dynamic Corporate Transformation — Graduate Course"
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="dct-hero">
<svg class="hero-svg" viewBox="0 0 560 420" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" aria-hidden="true">
  <g fill="none" stroke="#7FC8A9" stroke-opacity="0.22" stroke-width="1">
    <ellipse cx="400" cy="150" rx="150" ry="86" transform="rotate(-18 400 150)"/>
    <ellipse cx="400" cy="150" rx="112" ry="62" transform="rotate(-18 400 150)"/>
    <ellipse cx="400" cy="150" rx="76" ry="40" transform="rotate(-18 400 150)"/>
    <ellipse cx="400" cy="150" rx="42" ry="21" transform="rotate(-18 400 150)"/>
  </g>
  <g stroke="#7FC8A9" stroke-opacity="0.12" stroke-width="1">
    <path d="M40 360 H540"/><path d="M40 300 H540"/><path d="M40 240 H540"/>
    <path d="M100 400 V60"/><path d="M180 400 V60"/><path d="M260 400 V60"/>
  </g>
  <path class="traj-path" d="M60 385 C 150 370, 175 330, 205 285 S 280 210, 330 190 S 385 165, 400 150"
        fill="none" stroke="#C8A24B" stroke-width="3.2" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  <circle cx="60" cy="385" r="6" fill="#EDE9DC"/>
  <circle cx="400" cy="150" r="7" fill="#C8A24B"/>
  <circle cx="400" cy="150" r="12" fill="none" stroke="#C8A24B" stroke-opacity="0.5"/>
  <text x="74" y="392" fill="#EDE9DC" font-family="STIX Two Text, serif" font-size="17" font-style="italic">x&#8320;</text>
  <text x="418" y="146" fill="#D9BE7A" font-family="STIX Two Text, serif" font-size="17" font-style="italic">x*</text>
  <text x="430" y="262" fill="#7FC8A9" fill-opacity="0.7" font-family="STIX Two Text, serif" font-size="13" font-style="italic">J(x,u) level sets</text>
</svg>
<div class="hero-copy">
<span class="eyebrow">Graduate course · 14 weeks · Two volumes</span>
<h1>The enterprise as a <em>dynamic system</em>. Transformation as an <em>optimization problem</em>.</h1>
<p>An evolving state governed by transformation operators, resourced by a capital
architecture, measured by a performance architecture, exposed through a risk
architecture — and steered, from <strong>x&#8320;</strong> to <strong>x*</strong>, by the
General Enterprise Optimization Problem.</p>
<a class="btn-hero" href="axiom.html">Open the AXIOM Laboratory</a>
<a class="btn-hero-outline" href="schedule.html">View the 14-week schedule</a>
</div>
</div>

<div class="stat-strip">
<div class="stat"><span class="n">32</span><span class="l">Chapters</span></div>
<div class="stat"><span class="n">2</span><span class="l">Volumes</span></div>
<div class="stat"><span class="n">14</span><span class="l">Weeks</span></div>
<div class="stat"><span class="n">301</span><span class="l">Learning outcomes</span></div>
<div class="stat"><span class="n">489</span><span class="l">Exercises</span></div>
<div class="stat"><span class="n">16</span><span class="l">AXIOM modules</span></div>
</div>

<div class="vol-arc">
<div class="vol"><span class="eyebrow">Volume I · Weeks 1–7</span>
<h3>Poses the problem</h3>
<p>Foundations and mathematical framework: states, operators, dynamics, and the five
enterprise architectures — culminating in the General Enterprise Optimization Problem.</p></div>
<div class="arc-arrow">&#10230;</div>
<div class="vol solve"><span class="eyebrow">Volume II · Weeks 8–14</span>
<h3>Solves it</h3>
<p>Convex, nonlinear, dynamic, optimal-control, stochastic, robust, multi-objective,
and AI-assisted solution theory — through digital twins and integrated case studies.</p></div>
</div>
```

## The four-part teaching system

The book is the controlling document of a chapter-aligned ecosystem: the two-volume
text, the Instructor's Manuals, this course website, and the **AXIOM computational
laboratory**. Every chapter page carries its Learning Outcome Statements, a reading
guide, an exercise index, its AXIOM modules, and three downloadable companions — a
lecture deck, a seeded Python notebook, and a seeded Excel workbook that agree by
construction.

## The chapters

```{=html}
<div class="part-band"><span class="eyebrow">Volume I · Part 1</span><h3>Foundations</h3></div>
<div class="ch-grid">
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v1ch01.html"><span class="wk">WK 1</span><span class="num">1</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Introduction to Dynamic Corporate Transformation</span><span class="meta">5 LOS · 16 EX · AXIOM-01</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v1ch02.html"><span class="wk">WK 1</span><span class="num">2</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Enterprise Systems and Transformation</span><span class="meta">6 LOS · 16 EX · AXIOM-02</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v1ch03.html"><span class="wk">WK 2</span><span class="num">3</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Mathematical Foundations</span><span class="meta">5 LOS · 16 EX · AXIOM-03</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v1ch04.html"><span class="wk">WK 2</span><span class="num">4</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Enterprise Modeling Principles</span><span class="meta">5 LOS · 16 EX · AXIOM-04</span></a>
</div>
<div class="part-band"><span class="eyebrow">Volume I · Part 2</span><h3>Mathematical Representation</h3></div>
<div class="ch-grid">
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v1ch05.html"><span class="wk">WK 3</span><span class="num">5</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Enterprise State Representation</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 18 EX · AXIOM-01</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v1ch06.html"><span class="wk">WK 3</span><span class="num">6</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Enterprise Transformation Operators</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 19 EX · AXIOM-06</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v1ch07.html"><span class="wk">WK 4</span><span class="num">7</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Dynamic Enterprise Systems</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 19 EX · AXIOM-07</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v1ch08.html"><span class="wk">WK 4</span><span class="num">8</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Stochastic Enterprise Dynamics</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 19 EX · AXIOM-08</span></a>
</div>
<div class="part-band"><span class="eyebrow">Volume I · Part 3</span><h3>Enterprise Architectures</h3></div>
<div class="ch-grid">
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v1ch09.html"><span class="wk">WK 5</span><span class="num">9</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Enterprise State Architecture</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 19 EX · AXIOM-09</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v1ch10.html"><span class="wk">WK 5</span><span class="num">10</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Enterprise Capital Architecture</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 19 EX · AXIOM-09</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v1ch11.html"><span class="wk">WK 5</span><span class="num">11</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Enterprise Performance Architecture</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 19 EX · AXIOM-11</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v1ch12.html"><span class="wk">WK 6</span><span class="num">12</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Enterprise Risk, Resilience, and Robustness Architecture</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 19 EX · AXIOM-12</span></a>
</div>
<div class="part-band"><span class="eyebrow">Volume I · Part 4</span><h3>Integration and Transition</h3></div>
<div class="ch-grid">
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v1ch13.html"><span class="wk">WK 6</span><span class="num">13</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Unified Enterprise Transformation Architecture</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 19 EX · AXIOM-13</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v1ch14.html"><span class="wk">WK 7</span><span class="num">14</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Mathematical Analysis of the Unified Enterprise Transformation Architecture</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 19 EX · AXIOM-14</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v1ch15.html"><span class="wk">WK 7</span><span class="num">15</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Mathematical Formulation of Enterprise Optimization</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 24 EX · AXIOM-15</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v1ch16.html"><span class="wk">WK 7</span><span class="num">16</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Synthesis, Future Directions, and Transition to Volume II</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 19 EX · AXIOM-16</span></a>
</div>
<div class="part-band"><span class="eyebrow">Volume II · Part 1</span><h3>Enterprise Optimization Foundations</h3></div>
<div class="ch-grid">
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v2ch01.html"><span class="wk">WK 8</span><span class="num">1</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Introduction to Enterprise Optimization</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 12 EX · AXIOM-01</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v2ch02.html"><span class="wk">WK 8</span><span class="num">2</span><span class="t" style="display:block">The General Enterprise Optimization Problem Revisited</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 12 EX · AXIOM-02</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v2ch03.html"><span class="wk">WK 9</span><span class="num">3</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Convex Enterprise Optimization</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 13 EX · AXIOM-03</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v2ch04.html"><span class="wk">WK 9</span><span class="num">4</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Nonlinear Enterprise Optimization</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 12 EX · AXIOM-04</span></a>
</div>
<div class="part-band"><span class="eyebrow">Volume II · Part 2</span><h3>Dynamic Enterprise Optimization</h3></div>
<div class="ch-grid">
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v2ch05.html"><span class="wk">WK 10</span><span class="num">5</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Dynamic Enterprise Optimization</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 12 EX · AXIOM-04</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v2ch06.html"><span class="wk">WK 10</span><span class="num">6</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Optimal Control of Enterprise Systems</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 12 EX · AXIOM-04</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v2ch07.html"><span class="wk">WK 11</span><span class="num">7</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Dynamic Programming for Enterprise Systems</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 12 EX · AXIOM-07</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v2ch08.html"><span class="wk">WK 11</span><span class="num">8</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Hamilton--Jacobi--Bellman Enterprise Framework</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 12 EX · AXIOM-08</span></a>
</div>
<div class="part-band"><span class="eyebrow">Volume II · Part 3</span><h3>Enterprise Optimization Under Uncertainty</h3></div>
<div class="ch-grid">
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v2ch09.html"><span class="wk">WK 11</span><span class="num">9</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Stochastic Enterprise Optimization</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 12 EX · AXIOM-09</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v2ch10.html"><span class="wk">WK 12</span><span class="num">10</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Neuro-Fuzzy Robust Enterprise Optimization</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 12 EX · AXIOM-10</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v2ch11.html"><span class="wk">WK 12</span><span class="num">11</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Distributionally Robust Enterprise Optimization</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 12 EX · AXIOM-11</span></a>
</div>
<div class="part-band"><span class="eyebrow">Volume II · Part 4</span><h3>Multi-Objective and Intelligent Enterprise Optimization</h3></div>
<div class="ch-grid">
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v2ch12.html"><span class="wk">WK 13</span><span class="num">12</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Multi-Objective Enterprise Optimization</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 12 EX · AXIOM-12</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v2ch13.html"><span class="wk">WK 13</span><span class="num">13</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Machine Learning for Enterprise Transformation</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 12 EX · AXIOM-13</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v2ch14.html"><span class="wk">WK 14</span><span class="num">14</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Artificial Intelligence for Enterprise Optimization</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 12 EX · AXIOM-14</span></a>
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v2ch15.html"><span class="wk">WK 14</span><span class="num">15</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Enterprise Digital Twins and Autonomous Enterprise Transformation</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 12 EX · AXIOM-15</span></a>
</div>
<div class="part-band"><span class="eyebrow">Volume II · Part 5</span><h3>Applications and Synthesis</h3></div>
<div class="ch-grid">
<a class="ch-card" href="chapters/v2ch16.html"><span class="wk">WK 14</span><span class="num">16</span><span class="t" style="display:block">Enterprise Applications and Integrated Transformation Case Studies</span><span class="meta">10 LOS · 12 EX · AXIOM-16</span></a>
</div>
```
